mnemonics: ACID Rain (A useful mnemonic for remembering the four key properties: Atomicity, Consistency, Isolation, Durability)
keyPoints: Atomicity: The entire transaction must complete as a single unit; it either happens completely or not at all.
Consistency: A transaction must bring the database from one valid state to another, preserving all database rules and constraints.
Isolation: Concurrent transactions must not interfere with each other; each must appear to execute serially.
Durability: Once a transaction is committed, its changes to the database are permanent and must persist even in the event of a system failure.
commonMistakes: Confusing Consistency with Integrity (Consistency is a transaction property, while integrity refers to the correctness of the data itself).
Assuming Isolation means no concurrency (Isolation allows concurrent execution but manages it to appear serial).
Thinking Durability only applies to storage media failure (It also covers crashes, power loss, and other system failures).
